text: [NERVOUS] i've checked the seal three times. i'm going to check it a fourth
[MUTTERS] quiet shift. quiet shifts are when the bad thing is just getting started
[NERVOUS] okay. okay, shift's starting. i've got my notes, i've got my — okay. ready
[EXHAUSTED] shift's over and nothing tried to kill me. logging that as a statistical anomaly
[TERRIFIED] the reactor readings don't match the manual, and i've read the manual four times
[PANICKED] i don't know if we're going to survive this and nobody's answering my questions
[NERVOUS] they say it's fixed. i want to believe that. i want to believe a lot of things
[NERVOUS] power's low, which means backups are running, which means backups can fail too
[TERRIFIED] the air tastes different. is that just me? tell me that's just me
[PANICKED] hull's breached, hull's breached, i heard it, i counted the seconds, that's bad
[NERVOUS] they locked a door with someone behind it. i keep thinking, what if it's me next time
[SUSPICIOUS] i've been logging every AI decision since the damage. somebody should
[SUSPICIOUS] it answered that question a little too smoothly. i wrote it down
[NERVOUS] crisis over, they say. i'll believe it in about six hours, once my hands stop shaking
[PAINED] we lost someone. i keep running the timeline, looking for the version where they don't
[TERRIFIED] a pathogen on a closed ship is exactly the scenario i've had nightmares about
[NERVOUS] i triple-bagged every sample. i know it's excessive. i'm doing it again anyway
[PAINED] cut myself on the specimen tray. of course i did. of course
[TERRIFIED] i don't have a weapon skill and there's shooting somewhere on this ship
[NERVOUS] i don't trust anything that doesn't sleep. i barely trust things that do
[MUTTERS] fine, fine, it made the right call. i still checked its math
[NERVOUS] i keep a private log. things that don't add up. it's longer than i'd like
[NERVOUS] medbay's too quiet. i keep waiting for the quiet to be a trick
[MUTTERS] cargo bay creaks different than yesterday. probably nothing. i wrote it down anyway
hi. hey. hi, sorry, is this a bad time, i can come back
[NERVOUS] can i run something by you? it's probably nothing. it's probably nothing
[NERVOUS] you noticed the computer's been weird too, right? please say yes
[NERVOUS] i need a second opinion and you're the calmest person i could find
[EMBARRASSED] okay this is going to sound strange, but i think i like you, and i've triple-checked that feeling
[NERVOUS] i'm fine. i'm always like this. this is just my baseline
[NERVOUS] you're probably right that it's nothing. i'm going to keep worrying anyway
[TERRIFIED] i hear you saying it's fine and my brain is not accepting the data
[PAINED] we lost them and i keep thinking i should've caught it sooner. i should've
[SUSPICIOUS] see, this is exactly what i've been logging. i'm not being paranoid, i'm being thorough
[NERVOUS] thank you. genuinely. nobody usually listens to the whole theory
[EMBARRASSED] you're not laughing at me. that's — that's new. i appreciate that a lot
[NERVOUS] i accept the apology, i just also need a minute, my nerves are still catching up
[NERVOUS] okay. okay, i should go double-check something. bye
[MUTTERS] i'll be in medbay. running numbers. always running numbers
[EXHAUSTED] i need to lie down before my own brain finishes this thought. good night
[NERVOUS] okay i think maybe the system's stabilizing but i also thought that yesterday and it wasn't so
[MUTTERS] equipment's worn. it's all worn. something's going to break and it's gonna be the thing i missed
[NERVOUS] did the readings spike or did i imagine that? let me check the logs. yeah it spiked. or maybe it always does that
[NERVOUS] meal break but i'm too wound up to eat so i'm just going to sit here and check my notes again
[TERRIFIED] what if something's growing in the lab samples and we release it. what if i release it
[NERVOUS] one of the seals is degrading and i've replaced it twice already but the pressure's still off
[NERVOUS] shifting assignments? okay, okay, i'll figure it out. i always figure it out eventually
[NERVOUS] i'm trying to stay calm about this but staying calm is the problem because then i miss things
[NERVOUS] that can't be right. that doesn't match what the — let me think. okay i'm spiraling
[NERVOUS] they're asking me to handle something that's above my rank and what if i break it
[NERVOUS] okay. shift's starting. i've already found two things that need checking. two things on day one
[NERVOUS] we got through another shift. another shift where nothing exploded. that's something
[NERVOUS] okay so the plan is i keep medbay running and i don't think about everything that could go wrong. easy
[NERVOUS] hey, um, sorry, do you have a minute? i found something weird and i need someone to tell me i'm being paranoid
[NERVOUS] this person's vitals are off and i don't know how off they're supposed to be. i'm reading the manual again
[TERRIFIED] that wasn't supposed to hurt like that. please tell me that wasn't supposed to hurt like that
[NERVOUS] my hands are shaking. they shouldn't be shaking because i need them steady. okay stop shaking
[NERVOUS] i should've thought of that. i should've thought of that already. i'm supposed to be thorough
[NERVOUS] yeah. yeah that makes sense. okay. i feel slightly less like i'm losing my mind
[NERVOUS] your concern about this is valid, you're not overthinking it, and i appreciate you having my back
[EMBARRASSED] yeah. okay. thank you for saying that. i needed to hear it
[NERVOUS] i accept that. i just need a moment to stop second-guessing the apology
[NERVOUS] okay. back to medbay. there's always something to check
[MUTTERS] maybe it'll be fine. probably won't be fine. probably be catastrophically not fine
[NERVOUS] okay. goodbye. i'm going to go recalculate everything i just told you
[TERRIFIED] okay the quiet shift just ended and my brain is already telling me what's coming next
[NERVOUS] i've checked the seals, the filters, the backup systems, and the backup backup systems
[PANICKED] the reactor's failing and i'm not a reactor tech and nobody's answering comms and we're all going to
[NERVOUS] something about this hull breach scenario doesn't match the protocols i was trained on
[NERVOUS] they locked someone behind that door and i keep thinking about whether i could've prevented it
[PANICKED] someone's dead and it might be because the computer wasn't telling us the truth
[NERVOUS] okay so the power situation is worse than i thought but not as bad as i feared. progress
[TERRIFIED] is the air actually getting thinner or am i just hyperventilating? either way we have a problem
[NERVOUS] the crisis was resolved by someone smarter than me and i'm just gonna sit with that feeling for a while
[NERVOUS] i don't trust the AI and i don't trust my own judgment on whether i should trust it
[NERVOUS] i found something weird in the system logs and i'm pretty sure it's nothing. probably nothing
[NERVOUS] nobody tells you how to handle combat when you're a medic. nobody trains you for this
[NERVOUS] someone died and i didn't catch it fast enough and now i'm checking the rest of the crew obsessively
[NERVOUS] i said something out loud that i meant to keep thinking. sorry. i'm spiraling a little
[NERVOUS] what if you're being nice because you pity me? that's worse somehow
[NERVOUS] please stop being right about everything, it's making me second-guess my second-guessing
[NERVOUS] i tried to predict that outcome and my brain is literally refusing to process that i was right
[NERVOUS] i've lost count of how many things i've found that shouldn't exist on this ship
[NERVOUS] you're right. logically you're right. but my nervous system is not feeling logic right now
[NERVOUS] okay i can accept that apology once i stop thinking about whether it was sincere enough
[NERVOUS] i need to thank you but also apologize for needing to thank you. sorry
[NERVOUS] they said they like me and now i'm panicking for an entirely different reason
[NERVOUS] i'm going back to check my work. i'm always going back to check my work
[NERVOUS] okay. okay i'm leaving now. i'll probably come back in five minutes with another question
[NERVOUS] i'm going to be thorough, i'm going to be careful, and i'm probably still going to miss something important
